extra.reasoning:
The property forbids (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) both being true.

rec_drug_a logic: only r2 [pri 50] sets it true, under (asthma AND ckd_dialysis); otherwise default false. So rec_drug_a = true exactly when (asthma AND ckd_dialysis).

rec_drug_b logic: r1 [pri 50] sets true under asthma; r4 [pri 90] sets false under (asthma AND ckd_dialysis); default false. When both r1 and r4 match (asthma AND ckd_dialysis), r4's higher priority (90 > 50) wins, forcing rec_drug_b = false.

Therefore, in the only scenario where rec_drug_a can be true (asthma AND ckd_dialysis), rec_drug_b is forced to false by r4. Conversely, rec_drug_b is true only when asthma is true and ckd_dialysis is false (so r4 does not fire), but then r2 cannot fire and rec_drug_a defaults to false. The two outputs can never be simultaneously true across any of the 32 input combinations. The property holds.
